---
title: "Social Protection Project"
subtitle: "Field Data Analysis — Group Project"
author:
  - name: "Carina"
  - name: "Liyuansheng"
  - name: "Natalia"
  - name: "Farhan"
date: today
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

## Introduction

In order for Field Data Analysis our group will analyze which social protection program is the most associate with the reducing poverty gap between Unconditional Cash Transfer and Conditional Cash Transfer.

### Theoretical Framework

### Research Objective and Research Questions

**Research Objective**

This study aims to examine the association between two major social assistance modalities — Unconditional Cash Transfers (UCT) and Conditional Cash Transfers (CCT) — and poverty gap reduction across countries, using cross-country program performance data from the World Bank's ASPIRE database. Recognizing that the observed relationship may be influenced by program design and country-level characteristics, the analysis controls for transfer adequacy, benefit incidence toward the poorest quintile, average transfer value, country income classification, and geographic region. By comparing the conditional associations of UCT and CCT coverage with poverty gap reduction, the study seeks to inform evidence-based policy recommendations on the relative effectiveness of each modality in reducing the poverty level.

**Research Questions**

1.  Is there a statistically significant correlation between UCT program coverage (total population, poorest quantile) and poverty gap reduction across countries in the ASPIRE dataset, after controlling for **benefit incidence, country income class, regional classification, other social protection intervention (school feeding, public works, and social care services)?**

2.  Is there a statistically significant correlation between CCT program coverage (total population, poorest quantile) and poverty gap reduction across countries in the ASPIRE dataset, after controlling for the same confounders?

3.  Which cash transfer modality — UCT or CCT — **shows a stronger association with poverty gap reduction**, and does this relationship differ **when coverage is measured among the poorest quintile versus the total population**?

## Data Sources

We use data provided by ASPIRE World Bank

## Step by step

### Library and Dataset

We add necessary packages and library to extract data from ASPIRE dataset

```{r}
#install.packages("here")
#install.packages("readxl")
#install.packages("stargazer")
library(stargazer)
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)


```

We call the data from the ASPIRE first, since the data format still in excel file and nested format we need to clean and format the data first.

```{r}

# Check available sheets before loading
raw_peek<- read_excel(here("data/PER-Table7-Coverage12-Programs.xlsx"),
                      n_max = 10,
                      col_names = FALSE   # don't let R guess headers
)

print(raw_peek, n = 10, width = Inf)

#let's also do for the indicator table
raw_peek_t1 <- read_excel(
  here("data/PER-Table1-Key-Indicators.xlsx"),
  n_max = 10,
  col_names = FALSE
)
print(raw_peek_t1, n = 10, width = Inf)


```

Format key indicator data (Table 1):

```{r}
# 17 columns total: 2 blank notes cols, then 15 data cols
# skip=3: row1=title, row2=main headers, row3=sub-headers → data starts row4
col_names_t1 <- c(
  "note",                    # col1: blank / "*New survey*" annotation
  "blank2",                  # col2: always blank
  "region",
  "country_code",
  "income_class",
  "country",
  "year",
  "coverage_pq",             # Coverage - Poorest quintile
  "coverage_total",          # Coverage - Total
  "benefit_incidence_pq",    # Benefit Incidence (Poorest 20%) - Poorest quintile
  "avg_transfer_total",      # Average transfer amount $/day PPP - Total
  "adequacy_pq",             # Adequacy - Poorest quintile
  "adequacy_total",          # Adequacy - Total
  "gini_reduction",          # Gini reduction due to SPL (%)
  "headcount_reduction",     # Poverty Headcount reduction (%)
  "poverty_gap_reduction",   # Poverty Gap reduction (%) <- OUTCOME VARIABLE
  "benefit_cost_ratio"       # Benefit-cost ratio
)

df_t1 <- read_excel(
  here("data/PER-Table1-Key-Indicators.xlsx"),
  skip = 3,
  col_names = col_names_t1,
  na = c("n.a.", "NA", "")
) |>
  filter(!is.na(country_code)) |>
  select(-note, -blank2) |>
  mutate(year = as.integer(year),
         across(coverage_pq:benefit_cost_ratio, as.numeric))

glimpse(df_t1)
sample(df_t1)
```

Now format Table 7 (Coverage of 12 Programs — UCT and CCT are here):

```{r}
# 34 columns total: 2 blank + 5 id cols + 1 blank sep + 12 programs x 2 (PQ, Total) + 3 blank seps
# skip=4: row1=title, row2=level1 categories, row3=program names, row4=PQ/Total labels → data starts row5
col_names_t7 <- c(
  "note", "blank2",                        # cols 1-2: blank
  "region", "country_code",               # cols 3-4
  "income_class", "country", "year",      # cols 5-7
  "sep1",                                 # col 8: blank separator
  "si_contrib_pensions_pq",   "si_contrib_pensions_total",   # cols 9-10: Contributory pensions
  "si_other_insurance_pq",    "si_other_insurance_total",    # cols 11-12: Other social insurance
  "sep2",                                 # col 13: blank separator
  "lm_passive_pq",            "lm_passive_total",            # cols 14-15: Passive labor market
  "lm_active_pq",             "lm_active_total",             # cols 16-17: Active labor market
  "sep3",                                 # col 18: blank separator
  "uct_pq",                   "uct_total",                   # cols 19-20: Cash transfers (UCT)
  "cct_pq",                   "cct_total",                   # cols 21-22: Conditional cash transfer (CCT)
  "sa_noncontrib_pensions_pq","sa_noncontrib_pensions_total",# cols 23-24
  "sa_food_inkind_pq",        "sa_food_inkind_total",        # cols 25-26
  "sa_school_feeding_pq",     "sa_school_feeding_total",     # cols 27-28
  "sa_public_works_pq",       "sa_public_works_total",       # cols 29-30
  "sa_fee_waivers_pq",        "sa_fee_waivers_total",        # cols 31-32
  "sa_other_pq",              "sa_other_total"               # cols 33-34
)

df_t7 <- read_excel(
  here("data/PER-Table7-Coverage12-Programs.xlsx"),
  skip = 4,
  col_names = col_names_t7,
  na = c("n.a.", "NA", "")
) |>
  filter(!is.na(country_code)) |>
  select(-note, -blank2, -starts_with("sep")) |>
  mutate(year = as.integer(year),
         across(si_contrib_pensions_pq:sa_other_total, as.numeric))

glimpse(df_t7)
sample(df_t7)
```

Join UCT/CCT coverage with poverty gap outcome:

```{r}
df_analysis <- df_t7 |>
  select(country_code, country, region, income_class, year,
         uct_pq, uct_total, cct_pq, cct_total,
         school_feeding_pq    = sa_school_feeding_pq,
         school_feeding_total = sa_school_feeding_total,
         public_works_pq      = sa_public_works_pq,
         public_works_total   = sa_public_works_total,
         social_care_pq       = sa_other_pq,
         social_care_total    = sa_other_total) |>
  left_join(
    df_t1 |> select(country_code, year, poverty_gap_reduction,
                    avg_transfer_amount = avg_transfer_total),
    by = c("country_code", "year")
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(poverty_gap_reduction))

glimpse(df_analysis)
nrow(df_analysis)
sample(df_analysis)

```

Drop rows with no UCT **and** no CCT data:

```{r}
df_analysis_clean <- df_analysis |>
  filter((!is.na(uct_pq) | !is.na(uct_total)) & (!is.na(cct_pq) | !is.na(cct_total))) |>
  mutate(across(c(school_feeding_pq, school_feeding_total,
                  public_works_pq,   public_works_total,
                  social_care_pq,    social_care_total),
                ~ replace_na(., 0)))

glimpse(df_analysis_clean)
nrow(df_analysis_clean)

```

### Cleaning dataset

### Descriptive Data

### Visualization

### Modeling

```{r}


# --- Poorest Quintile models ---
m1_pq <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~ avg_transfer_amount + uct_pq + cct_pq,
            data = df_analysis_clean)

m2_pq <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~ avg_transfer_amount + uct_pq + cct_pq +
              factor(income_class) + factor(region),
            data = df_analysis_clean)

m3_pq <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~ avg_transfer_amount + uct_pq + cct_pq +
              factor(income_class) + factor(region) +
              school_feeding_pq + public_works_pq,
            data = df_analysis_clean)

m4_pq <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~ avg_transfer_amount + uct_pq + cct_pq +
              factor(income_class) + factor(region) +
              school_feeding_pq + public_works_pq + social_care_pq,
            data = df_analysis_clean)

# --- Total Population models ---
m1_tot <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~ avg_transfer_amount + uct_total + cct_total,
             data = df_analysis_clean)

m2_tot <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~ avg_transfer_amount + uct_total + cct_total +
               factor(income_class) + factor(region),
             data = df_analysis_clean)

m3_tot <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~ avg_transfer_amount + uct_total + cct_total +
               factor(income_class) + factor(region) +
               school_feeding_total + public_works_total,
             data = df_analysis_clean)

m4_tot <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~ avg_transfer_amount + uct_total + cct_total +
               factor(income_class) + factor(region) +
               school_feeding_total + public_works_total + social_care_total,
             data = df_analysis_clean)
```

**Table 1 — Poorest Quintile Coverage**

```{r}
#| results: asis
stargazer(m1_pq, m2_pq, m3_pq, m4_pq,
          type             = "latex",
          header           = FALSE,
          title            = "Poverty Gap Reduction --- Poorest Quintile Coverage",
          column.labels    = c("Model 1", "Model 2", "Model 3", "Model 4"),
          dep.var.labels   = "Poverty Gap Reduction (\\%)",
          single.row       = TRUE,
          font.size        = "tiny",
          column.sep.width = "1pt",
          no.space         = TRUE)
```

**Table 2 — Total Population Coverage**

```{r}
#| results: asis
stargazer(m1_tot, m2_tot, m3_tot, m4_tot,
          type             = "latex",
          header           = FALSE,
          title            = "Poverty Gap Reduction --- Total Population Coverage",
          column.labels    = c("Model 1", "Model 2", "Model 3", "Model 4"),
          dep.var.labels   = "Poverty Gap Reduction (\\%)",
          single.row       = TRUE,
          font.size        = "tiny",
          column.sep.width = "1pt",
          no.space         = TRUE)
```

## Expenditure-Based Analysis (Dataset 2)

This section uses a second dataset combining poverty gap reduction from Table 1 (filtered to 2020–2022) with Social Assistance expenditure as % of GDP from the ASPIRE EXP-SA table. Unlike the coverage-based models above, regressors here measure how much governments *spend* on each programme rather than how many people are reached.

### Load and prepare Dataset 2

```{r}
df2 <- read.csv(here("data/clean_dataset2.csv"))

# Replace NA in confounder expenditure cols with 0 (no reported spend = no prog)
# Keep only rows where at least one of the key regressors is observed
df2_model <- df2 |>
  filter(!is.na(exp_total_sa_excl_hlt) |
           !is.na(exp_cct) |
           !is.na(exp_uct)) |>
  mutate(
    across(c(exp_school_feeding, exp_public_works, exp_social_care),
           ~ replace_na(., 0)),
    income_class = droplevels(factor(income_class)),
    region       = droplevels(factor(region))
  )

cat("Rows available for modelling:", nrow(df2_model), "\n")
glimpse(df2_model)
```

### Staircase regression — Expenditure (% GDP)

```{r}
e1 <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~
           exp_total_sa_excl_hlt + exp_cct + exp_uct,
         data = df2_model)

e2 <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~
           exp_total_sa_excl_hlt + exp_cct + exp_uct +
           income_class + region,
         data = df2_model)

e3 <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~
           exp_total_sa_excl_hlt + exp_cct + exp_uct +
           income_class + region +
           exp_school_feeding + exp_public_works,
         data = df2_model)

e4 <- lm(poverty_gap_reduction ~
           exp_total_sa_excl_hlt + exp_cct + exp_uct +
           income_class + region +
           exp_school_feeding + exp_public_works + exp_social_care,
         data = df2_model)
```

**Table 3 — Poverty Gap Reduction: Expenditure (% GDP)**

```{r}
#| results: asis
stargazer(e1, e2, e3, e4,
          type             = "latex",
          header           = FALSE,
          title            = "Poverty Gap Reduction --- SA Expenditure (\\% GDP)",
          column.labels    = c("Model 1", "Model 2", "Model 3", "Model 4"),
          dep.var.labels   = "Poverty Gap Reduction (\\%)",
          covariate.labels = c(
            "Total SA Exp. excl. Health (\\% GDP)",
            "CCT Expenditure (\\% GDP)",
            "UCT Expenditure (\\% GDP)",
            "School Feeding Exp. (\\% GDP)",
            "Public Works Exp. (\\% GDP)",
            "Social Care Exp. (\\% GDP)"
          ),
          omit             = c("income_class", "region"),
          add.lines        = list(
            c("Income class FE", "No",  "Yes", "Yes", "Yes"),
            c("Region FE",       "No",  "Yes", "Yes", "Yes")
          ),
          single.row       = TRUE,
          font.size        = "tiny",
          column.sep.width = "1pt",
          no.space         = TRUE)
```

## Conclusion

## Ethics Disclosure

## Reference
